headlines.title: Venezuela Oil Exports Hit Seven-Year High
headlines.summary: Venezuela’s oil exports rose to 1.23 million barrels per day (bpd) in April, the highest level since 2018, as shipments to the United States, India, and Europe accelerated. headlines.title: US Oil Drillers See Modest Uptick in Activity
headlines.summary: The total number of active drilling rigs for oil and gas in the United States rose this week, according to new data that Baker Hughes published on Friday, bringing the total rig count in the US to 547, down 37 from this same time last year. headlines.title: Chevron Earnings Top Estimates While Refining Swings to Loss
headlines.summary: Chevron beat first-quarter expectations as higher oil prices lifted upstream earnings, offsetting a sharp swing to losses in refining. headlines.title: Oil Rally Stalls as Iran Proposal Triggers Profit-Taking
headlines.summary: Oil prices head for a weekly close below $110 after wild swings, as Iran’s negotiation proposal cools markets while uncertainty over Trump’s war-powers deadline keeps traders on edge. headlines.title: Kazakhstan to Stick With OPEC+ After UAE Exit
headlines.summary: Defying some analysts’ expectations, Kazakhstan has opted not to follow the United Arab Emirates in abandoning OPEC’s oil production quotas. The Kazakh Energy Ministry announced April 29 that “the issue of changing the format of the country’s participation in the alliance is not

headlines.title: Syria Still Leans on Russia for Oil Supply
headlines.summary: Russia remains Syria’s top oil supplier and has even boosted its sales so far this year despite the fact that the Middle Eastern country emerged from a 14-year-long civil war leaning West and deposing Russia-backed leader Bashar al-Assad. headlines.title: Ukraine Hits Russian Black Sea Oil Port Four Times in One Week
headlines.summary: Russia's Black Sea port of Tuapse was targeted and hit by a new Ukrainian drone strike early on Friday, in the fourth attack on the port in just one week as Ukraine looks to limit Russia's oil exports and revenues amid soaring crude prices. headlines.title: High Gas Prices Are Just the Start of a Much Deeper Energy Crunch
headlines.summary: When a major oil shock hits, most Americans notice it first at the pump. That’s exactly what’s happening now. headlines.title: Exxon Beats Q1 Earnings on Oil Price Surge Despite 6% Production Drop
headlines.summary: Higher oil prices drove the first-quarter adjusted earnings at ExxonMobil (NYSE: XOM) above the analyst estimates as the jump in prices more than offset lower oil and gas production in the Middle East and Kazakhstan. headlines.title: Pakistan Imports First LNG Cargo in Weeks in Relief for Energy Crisis
headlines.summary: Pakistan has welcomed the first LNG cargo in almost two months as an LNG carrier docked at the GasPort terminal in what would be the first relief to the energy crisis in the country.
